decisionGuides.question: Choose one value from a known set

when: The number of options is small and should stay visible.
Fast switching matters more than compactness.
The choices can stay in a dropdown.
Search is not required.
The user needs filtering or type-to-search.
The option list can become long.
All options should be visible with classic form semantics.
You need explicit radio semantics in a form.
The choice should be presented as richer rows with description or metadata.
The options are hierarchical.
Selected values should collapse into tags.
The user needs filtering and a compact control.
The option set is small and visible.
A segmented-control style is appropriate.
notes: Set `selectionMode="multiple"`.

when: The options can stay visible as a checklist.
Multi-selection must work across a hierarchy.
Set `selectionMode="multiple"`.

decisionGuides.question: Show contextual or blocking UI above the page

when: The content is a short label or hint.
The panel should not be interactive.
The content is anchored, custom, and non-blocking.
The panel can contain controls.
You need a lightweight action menu or menu-like list.
The menu is triggered by right click or context action.
The task needs a larger side panel but should keep page context visible.
The task is blocking and requires focused attention.
The user is confirming or canceling a destructive or high-risk action.
The message should stay visible in the page layout.
The message is ephemeral and should not shift layout.
A root `TToastProvider` is already mounted.
The user needs a purposeful zero-state, not only a status message.
You need a determinate progress indicator.
You need indeterminate loading feedback.
You want loading placeholders that preserve layout.
The story is a single headline number, optionally with a trend and delta.
It is not a plot — prefer the number over a chart when one value says it.
You want an inline micro-trend next to a value, in a table cell, or in text.
No axes, legend, or tooltip are needed.
You need line, area, or bar plots with axes, gridlines, legend, and hover tooltip.
There are one or more series over shared categories.
The data is a part-of-whole breakdown (donut or pie).
You are showing completion toward a single target, not a distribution or trend.

recipes.name: Field with text input
useWhen: A form control needs label, hint, and validation messaging.
notes: Match `htmlFor` on `TFormField` with the child `id`.
Mirror invalid state onto `TInput` when error text is shown.

recipes.name: Searchable multi-choice field
useWhen: A form needs compact multi-select with filtering.
notes: Pass option keywords when search should match hidden synonyms.
Use `maxVisibleTags` to avoid oversized triggers.

recipes.name: Scheduling pair
useWhen: A form needs start and end scheduling controls.
notes: Keep both values in `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` format.
Set end `min` from the selected start time when appropriate.

recipes.name: Full-viewport application shell
useWhen: The app needs a header plus side navigation that fills the whole screen and adapts to mobile.
The layout must use the full page width on large monitors, not a centered column.
notes: `TAppShell` orchestrates the header, sidebar, and fluid content in one component; put a `TNavMenu` in its `sidebar` slot and it inherits collapsed state automatically.
Leave `mobile` unset to auto-switch at `breakpoint` (default 768px), or bind it to force mobile; the sidebar becomes an off-canvas drawer with a header menu button.
Mount it at the app root. Wrap only inner regions in `TContainer` if a specific area needs a constrained reading width.

recipes.name: Sidebar application shell
useWhen: The screen needs persistent section navigation and you are composing the shell by hand.
notes: Let `TNavMenu` inherit collapsed state from `TSidebar` when possible.
Prefer `TAppShell` (recipe `app-shell`) when you also need full-viewport sizing and a responsive mobile drawer.

recipes.name: Dashboard metric grid
useWhen: A dashboard needs KPI cards.
notes: Use `TCard` only when the stat needs additional arbitrary content.

recipes.name: Color field
useWhen: A form needs a color selection control without adding a dedicated TreeUI component.
notes: Wrap the native color input with `TFormField` for label, hint, and error handling.

recipes.name: Tag input field
useWhen: A form needs freeform tag entry such as domains, labels, or emails.
notes: Keep the draft value in app state and add a tag on Enter or separator keys.
Use removable `TTag` items below or inside the field wrapper.

recipes.name: Data table with paging
useWhen: A structured list needs external paging controls.
notes: `TTable` stays focused on structured listing; filters and toolbars compose around it.
Use `#cell-{key}` slots when a column renders derived or rich content.

recipes.name: Router-backed tabs
useWhen: Tabs should deep-link to app state without turning into primary route navigation.
notes: Bind `v-model` to a route param, query param, or derived route name in the app layer.
Prefer `TNavMenu` when the selection becomes app-section navigation instead of in-place panel switching.

recipes.name: Destructive confirmation flow
useWhen: The user is confirming a high-risk action.
notes: Use `TToast` only for the post-confirm success feedback.

recipes.name: Chart surface
useWhen: A screen needs data visualization inside a framed, titled surface.
notes: Prefer the native TChart / TDonutChart / TSparkline — no external charting library is required.
Use TreeUI for the surrounding surface, headings, badges, range filters, and empty/loading states.
TChart has its own loading and empty slots; you can still wrap it in TCard for the frame.
Only reach for an external chart engine for chart types TreeUI does not cover yet (e.g. scatter, heatmap).

recipes.name: Metric dashboard
useWhen: A dashboard shows KPIs alongside trends and breakdowns.
notes: Lead with TStat tiles; embed a TSparkline in a stat's meta slot for the inline trend.
Use one TChart for the main time series and a TDonutChart for a part-of-whole split.
Keep series colors stable across the dashboard — rely on the --tree-color-chart-* order, do not remap by rank.

recipes.name: Anchored help affordance
useWhen: A trigger needs short or medium contextual help.
notes: Use `TTooltip` for short, non-interactive help.
Escalate to `TPopover` when the content becomes interactive.

recipes.name: Pricing comparison section
useWhen: Plans should be compared side by side.
notes: Use `highlighted` and `badge` on the recommended plan.

recipes.name: Hierarchical selection
useWhen: Selection works across nested groups or folders.
notes: Use `selectionMode="multiple"` when permissions or tag assignment spans nested nodes.

guidance: Use the plugin when global registration is preferred.
Use named imports when tree-shaken local imports fit the consumer app better.
Prefer `T<Name>` imports in all new code.

globalRules: Always import `@treeui/vue/style.css` once in the consuming application.
All components use the `T<Name>` export prefix; there are no `Tree<Name>` aliases.
Overlay-style components generally support controlled and uncontrolled open state.

required: Mount one `TToastProvider` near the application root before calling `useToast()` from feature components.
notes: Use `useToast()` for programmatic notifications.
Choose provider `position` once per app shell unless a feature has a strong reason to differ.

required: Wrap a control such as `TInput`, `TSelect`, `TCombobox`, `TCheckbox`, or `TDatePicker`.
Pass `htmlFor` matching the child control `id` when the child exposes one.
notes: Set the child control invalid state separately when `error` is present.
Pass `options` as `{ label, value, disabled? }[]` where `value` can be `string` or `number`.
Use `placeholder` when no selection has been made.
Pass `options` as labeled items and keep labels human-readable because search matches visible fields.
Pass `modelValue` as `string[]`.
Option search checks `label`, `description`, and `keywords`.
Use `maxVisibleTags` to control trigger density in compact layouts.
Use `selectionMode="single"` for segmented single choice.
Use `selectionMode="multiple"` for small visible multi-choice sets.
Bind `modelValue` to a `YYYY-MM-DD` string.
Use `min` and `max` with the same format.
Bind `modelValue` to a `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` string.
Use `step` in seconds. Values divisible by 60 produce minute increments.
Use `min` and `max` in the same date-time format.
Bind `modelValue` to `File[]`.
Use `accept`, `multiple`, and size limits in the app layer to constrain uploads.
Mount at the app root; it fills the viewport (100dvh, full width) and scrolls its content region independently.
Put navigation in the `sidebar` slot (e.g. `TNavMenu`) — it inherits collapsed state from the shell provider like `TSidebar`.
Leave `mobile` unset to auto-switch to an off-canvas drawer at `breakpoint`, or bind `mobile` to control it explicitly.
Use `v-model:collapsed` for the desktop rail and `v-model:sidebarOpen` for the mobile drawer when the app persists layout state.
Pair with `TNavMenu` for app-section navigation.
Use controlled `collapsed` state when the app shell persists layout preferences.
Prefer `TAppShell` when you also want full-viewport sizing, a top header, and a responsive mobile drawer.
When nested inside `TSidebar` or `TAppShell`, `collapsed` can be inherited from the sidebar provider.
Use `nodes` shaped as `{ id, label, description?, meta?, disabled?, children? }[]`.
Control `expanded` separately when the parent app needs to persist open branches.
Pass explicit `status` per item only when the app should override the computed complete/current/upcoming logic.
Pass `plans` matching the same core fields used by `TPricingCard`.
Use `v-model:open` for the idiomatic Vue controlled pattern.
Use `href` for native links.
Use `to` when the app has vue-router and breadcrumb items should render RouterLink.
Pass `series` as `{ name, data, color? }[]` and `labels` as a parallel `string[]` (one label per data index).
Set `type` to `line`, `area`, or `bar`; use `stacked` only with `bar`.
Series colors come from `--tree-color-chart-1..8` in order; pass `color` per series to override — do not remap colors when the series set changes.
Pass `valueFormat: (n) => string` to format axis and tooltip values (currency, compact, etc.).
No charting library or provider is required — the SVG and geometry are built in.
Pass `data` as `number[]`; there are no axes, legend, or tooltip by design.
Size it with `width`/`height`; use it inside `TStat`, table cells, or text.
Always keep a visible number nearby — a sparkline supplements a value, it does not replace it.
Pass `segments` as `{ label, value, color? }[]`; non-positive values are dropped.
Set `thickness: 0` for a full pie; keep the default for a donut with a center readout.
Use the `center` slot (donut only) for a custom center; otherwise it shows the total or the hovered slice.

components.name: TAccordion
components.purpose: Organized content sections with expandable/collapsible behavior. Supports single-open and multi-open modes.
a11y: attributes: {"trigger":["aria-expanded (reflects open state)","aria-controls (references panel id)"],"panel":["role=\"region\"","aria-labelledby (references trigger id)"]}

keyboard: [{"key":"Enter / Space","action":"Toggle the focused item"},{"key":"ArrowDown","action":"Move focus to the next item trigger"},{"key":"ArrowUp","action":"Move focus to the previous item trigger"},{"key":"Home","action":"Move focus to the first item trigger"},{"key":"End","action":"Move focus to the last item trigger"}]

useWhen: Sections expand and collapse inside the page flow.
Multiple disclosure items share one layout.
avoidWhen: You need tabbed navigation or route-level navigation.

alternatives.when: Only one panel should be active and the tab labels must stay visible.
The content is hierarchical rather than just collapsible.

components.name: TAlert
components.purpose: Inline status banner for info, success, warning, and danger messages.

events.name: dismiss
events.description: Emitted when the dismiss button is clicked.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Alert message content.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Optional leading icon; rendered with aria-hidden.

slots.name: dismiss-icon
slots.description: Custom dismiss button content; replaces the default × icon.

a11y: keyboard: Tab to dismiss button, Enter/Space to activate

useWhen: Feedback should stay visible in-flow.
The message may contain actions or supporting content.
avoidWhen: The feedback should disappear automatically.

alternatives.when: The message is ephemeral and should not affect layout.
The screen has no data and needs stronger guidance.

components.name: TAppShell
components.purpose: Full-viewport application shell with a top header, a persistent side navigation, and a fluid content area that collapses the sidebar into an off-canvas drawer on small screens.

props.name: showCollapseButton
props.note: Renders the built-in collapse toggle in the sidebar footer. Desktop only, and only when `collapsible` is set — no need to wire your own.

useWhen: You need a full-viewport application layout with a header, a side navigation, and a fluid content area.
The layout should use the entire page width (not a centered max-width) and switch the sidebar to an off-canvas drawer on small screens.
avoidWhen: You only need one region (just a top bar, or just a side rail) — compose TNavbar or TSidebar directly.
The page content should be centered with a readable max width — use TContainer inside the shell content instead.

alternatives.when: You want a standalone collapsible side rail without the header/content orchestration or responsive drawer.
You only need the top application bar.
You need a general temporary overlay panel rather than the shell's responsive navigation.

notes: Mount at the app root; it fills the viewport (100dvh, full width) and scrolls its content region independently.
Put navigation in the `sidebar` slot (e.g. `TNavMenu`) — it inherits collapsed state from the shell provider like `TSidebar`.
Leave `mobile` unset to auto-switch to an off-canvas drawer at `breakpoint`, or bind `mobile` to control it explicitly.
Use `v-model:collapsed` for the desktop rail and `v-model:sidebarOpen` for the mobile drawer when the app persists layout state.

components.name: TAvatar
components.description: User image with fallback initials and optional status indicator dot.

props.name: src
props.description: URL of the avatar image.

props.name: alt
props.description: Accessible label for the avatar. Also used to derive initials if `initials` is not set.

props.name: initials
props.description: Explicit initials to display when no image is provided. Overrides derived initials from `alt`.

props.name: status
props.description: Shows a colored status dot on the avatar.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Optional overlay content inside the avatar.

a11y: status_dot: Has its own `aria-label` with the status value.

components.name: TBadge
components.purpose: Compact status or metadata label with optional icon.

props.name: tone
props.description: Optional semantic tone layered on top of the visual variant.

components.name: TBreadcrumb
components.purpose: Hierarchical navigation trail showing the user's current location within a site or application.

props.name: separator
props.description: Character or string used to separate breadcrumb items.

notes: Use `href` for native links.
Use `to` when the app has vue-router and breadcrumb items should render RouterLink.

components.name: TButton
components.purpose: Clickable action trigger with variants, sizes, optional polymorphism, and loading state.

props.name: variant
props.note: `brand` is a button-only, high-emphasis gradient variant driven by the `--tree-gradient-brand` token. Use it for the single primary CTA of a screen.

props.name: loading
props.note: loading implies disabled behavior

props.name: block
props.note: Stretches the button to the full width of its container. Useful for menu rows and mobile CTAs.

props.name: align
props.note: Content alignment; only meaningful together with `block`. Use `start` for left-aligned icon+label menu rows.

components.name: TCard
components.purpose: Surface container with optional header and footer slots.

props.name: title
props.description: Optional card heading rendered inside the header area.

useWhen: You need a framed surface for related content.
The layout can be composed from slots instead of a dedicated domain component.
avoidWhen: You only need width constraint or spacing.

alternatives.when: The main need is width constraint.
The main need is spacing between children.

components.name: TChart
components.purpose: Lightweight cartesian chart (line, area, bar) for multi-series data, with axes, gridlines, hover tooltip, legend, and an accessible data table. No external charting dependency.

props.name: series
props.note: Each item is { name: string; data: number[]; color?: string }.

props.name: labels
props.note: X-axis categories, aligned index-for-index to each series' data.

props.name: height
props.note: Width is fluid (measured via ResizeObserver).

props.name: smooth
props.note: Curved line/area.

props.name: stacked
props.note: Stacks bar series; ignored for line/area. Negative values are clamped to 0 in stacked mode (grouped mode renders them).

props.name: showLegend
props.default: auto (true when more than one series)

props.name: showPoints
props.note: Always draw markers on line/area points.

props.name: animated
props.note: Draw-in animation; disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

events.name: point-click
events.payload: { index: number; label: string; values: { name: string; value: number; color: string }[] }

a11y: SVG is aria-hidden; a visually-hidden <table> mirrors the series data for assistive tech.
Legend defaults on for multiple series so identity is not color-only.
Provide a meaningful ariaLabel describing what the chart shows.

useWhen: You need a line, area, or bar chart with axes, gridlines, legend, and a hover tooltip.
One or more series share a set of category labels.
avoidWhen: A single value tells the story — use TStat.
You only need an inline trend with no axes — use TSparkline.
The data is a part-of-whole breakdown — use TDonutChart.

alternatives.when: The trend should be inline and axis-less.
The data is proportional (parts of a whole).

notes: Pass `series` as `{ name, data, color? }[]` and `labels` as a parallel `string[]` (one label per data index).
Set `type` to `line`, `area`, or `bar`; use `stacked` only with `bar`.
Series colors come from `--tree-color-chart-1..8` in order; pass `color` per series to override — do not remap colors when the series set changes.
Pass `valueFormat: (n) => string` to format axis and tooltip values (currency, compact, etc.).
No charting library or provider is required — the SVG and geometry are built in.

components.name: TCheckbox
components.purpose: Boolean toggle with indeterminate state; supports v-model via modelValue.

useWhen: Each option is independent.
You need standard checkbox form semantics.
avoidWhen: Exactly one option must be selected.

alternatives.when: Only one option is valid.
The choice is a single immediate on/off state.

components.name: TColorSwatch
components.purpose: Pick a colour from a row of preset swatches, with an optional native colour input for an arbitrary value. Built for accent/brand pickers.

props.name: modelValue
props.note: Selected colour as a hex string. Matching is case-insensitive.

props.name: options
props.note: Preset swatches, each `{ label, value }`. `label` becomes the button's accessible name.

props.name: allowCustom
props.note: Appends a native `<input type="color">` for values outside the presets.

props.name: label
props.note: Accessible label for the swatch group (`role="group"`).

a11y: Each swatch is a native button with `aria-pressed` reflecting selection and `aria-label` from the option label.
Selection is conveyed by an outline (not colour alone), since the swatch's own colour is the content.

components.name: TCombobox
components.purpose: Searchable combobox / autocomplete input with local filtering, controlled selection, and shared open-state behavior.

props.name: options
props.note: Array of { label: string; value: string; description?: string; keywords?: string[]; disabled?: boolean }

useWhen: The user needs to filter or search a single-choice list.
A dropdown is appropriate, but plain select is too limited.
avoidWhen: Search is unnecessary and the option list is short.

alternatives.when: You only need a standard dropdown without filtering.
The user must select multiple values.

notes: Pass `options` as labeled items and keep labels human-readable because search matches visible fields.

components.name: TConfirmDialog
components.purpose: High-level confirmation dialog built on the shared modal contract, with default confirm and cancel actions.

components.name: TContainer
components.purpose: Width-constrained layout wrapper for readable page content and app sections.
a11y: Acts as a structural wrapper only; semantics come from the `as` prop and child content.

useWhen: You want consistent max width and horizontal page padding.
The page shell should stay full-width while content stays readable.
avoidWhen: You need internal spacing between children.
You are laying out a whole screen — reach for TPage, which adds page padding and vertical rhythm.

alternatives.when: You want a full page region with block padding and vertical spacing between sections, not just a width constraint.
The main need is vertical or horizontal spacing.
The main need is responsive columns.

components.name: TContextMenu

props.name: items
props.description: Menu items to render.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state (v-model:open).

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Uncontrolled initial open state.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Prevents the context menu from opening.

props.name: size
props.description: Item sizing variant.

events.name: select
events.description: Emitted when a menu item is selected.

events.name: update:open
events.description: Emitted when open state changes (v-model support).

slots.name: default
slots.description: Content that acts as the right-click trigger area.

slots.name: item
slots.description: Custom rendering for each menu item.

a11y: keyboard: ["Right-click / contextmenu event opens menu at cursor","ArrowDown / ArrowUp navigates items","Home / End jumps to first / last enabled item","Enter / Space selects focused item","Escape closes menu and restores focus"]
aria: ["aria-disabled on disabled items","aria-label on menu element"]
focus: ["First enabled item receives focus on open","Focus restored to trigger area on close"]
behavior: Opens at cursor position on right-click (contextmenu event).
Uses Teleport to body to escape stacking contexts.
Closes on click outside, Escape key, or item selection.
Prevents native browser context menu on trigger area.
Uses position fixed for viewport-relative placement.

useWhen: Actions are triggered from context-click or long-press style behavior.
avoidWhen: The menu is triggered from a standard button.

alternatives.when: A normal trigger button opens the menu.

components.name: TDatePicker
components.purpose: Date input with calendar popover and keyboard navigation.

useWhen: The value is a date without time.
avoidWhen: Time selection matters.

alternatives.when: The value must include time.

notes: Bind `modelValue` to a `YYYY-MM-DD` string.
Use `min` and `max` with the same format.

components.name: TDateTimePicker
components.purpose: Date and time picker with a calendar grid, time selection, and overlay trigger.

props.name: modelValue
props.note: Uses `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm`.

props.name: step
props.note: Seconds between time options.

useWhen: The value must include both date and time.
The user should pick a schedule from one compact control.
avoidWhen: Only a day is needed.

alternatives.when: Only the date matters.

notes: Bind `modelValue` to a `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm` string.
Use `step` in seconds. Values divisible by 60 produce minute increments.
Use `min` and `max` in the same date-time format.

components.name: TDivider

props.name: decorative
props.description: When true, renders role='none' (purely visual). When false, renders as role='separator'.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for non-decorative dividers.

a11y: aria-label: Set from `label` prop when non-decorative.

components.name: TDonutChart
components.purpose: Part-of-whole chart rendered as a donut (default) or full pie, with a legend, percentages, an interactive center readout, and an accessible label.

props.name: segments
props.note: Each item is { label: string; value: number; color?: string }. Non-positive values are omitted.

props.name: size
props.note: Square canvas in px.

props.name: thickness
props.note: Ring thickness; 0 renders a full pie.

props.name: gap
props.note: Gap between slices, in degrees.

props.name: rounded
props.note: Round the ends of donut segments (widens the gap to make room). Best with a thinner ring.

props.name: centerLabel
props.note: Donut only; ignored for pie.

a11y: Renders role=img with an aria-label summarizing the chart.
Legend + percentages give a non-visual reading of each slice.

useWhen: You are showing how a total splits into a handful of categories.
A donut center readout (total or hovered slice) is useful.
avoidWhen: There are many categories or precise comparison matters — use TChart bars.
The data is a trend over time — use TChart or TSparkline.

alternatives.when: Precise magnitude comparison across categories is needed (bars).

notes: Pass `segments` as `{ label, value, color? }[]`; non-positive values are dropped.
Set `thickness: 0` for a full pie; keep the default for a donut with a center readout.
Use the `center` slot (donut only) for a custom center; otherwise it shows the total or the hovered slice.

components.name: TDrawer
components.purpose: Side-anchored panel for secondary content, navigation, or forms.

useWhen: The user needs a larger contextual panel while keeping page context visible.
avoidWhen: The task requires full modal focus or only a small anchored panel.

alternatives.when: The task should be fully blocking.
A compact anchored panel is enough.

components.name: TDropdown
components.purpose: Trigger + floating menu with keyboard navigation for action lists.

props.name: items
props.note: Array of { label: string; value: string; disabled?: boolean }

props.name: align
props.note: Horizontal alignment of the menu relative to the trigger. Use `end` when the trigger sits near the right edge of a clipping container (e.g. a table actions column).

useWhen: A button should open a short list of actions or choices.
avoidWhen: The user is choosing a form value with v-model.

alternatives.when: The dropdown is a form control for selecting a value.
The menu opens from a context action.

components.name: TEmptyState
components.purpose: Structured empty, no-results, and first-run placeholder block with optional icon, body content, and actions.

useWhen: A screen or section has no content and needs guidance.
avoidWhen: You only need a brief inline warning.

alternatives.when: The layout already has content and only needs a message.

components.name: TFileUpload
components.purpose: Composable dropzone and file picker with browse, drag-and-drop, and Ctrl+V paste support, plus per-file upload state: progress, remaining time, thumbnails, and independent per-file retry/resume.

props.name: disabled
props.description: The ONLY prop that gates interaction. Disables the dropzone, the native input, Clear all, and every per-row Remove/Retry.

props.name: loading
props.description: Presentational only. Applies `is-loading`, `aria-busy` on the dropzone, and the corner spinner. It gates NO interaction — see behavior.loading_is_presentational.

props.name: description
props.default: Click to browse, drag and drop, or paste with Ctrl+V.

props.name: uploadState
props.description: Per-file transport state, keyed by File OBJECT IDENTITY. Accepts a ReadonlyMap<File, TFileUploadState> or a (file, index) => TFileUploadState | undefined resolver. Omit it and the component renders exactly as before: no bars, no status lines, no retry.

props.name: dragLabel
props.description: Replaces `label` in the dropzone while an acceptable drag is over it. Set it to the same value as `label` to opt out of the text swap.

props.name: dragRejectLabel
props.default: This file type is not accepted
props.description: Replaces `label` while a drag that cannot work is over the dropzone. Only reachable when drag-reject detection is possible — see behavior.drag_reject_detection_is_mime_only.

props.name: thumbnails
props.description: Mint object-URL previews and decode dimensions for image/* files. Set false when you always supply `state.thumbnailUrl` yourself.

props.name: maxThumbnailSize
props.description: Skip auto-thumbnailing above this byte size. null removes the cap.

props.name: resumeLabel
props.description: Receives Math.round(state.progress). Used only when state.resumable === true AND state.progress is a number.

props.name: remainingTimeFormat
props.description: Formats state.remainingMs. Ignored for a row that supplies state.remainingLabel.

props.name: statusLabels
props.description: MERGED over the defaults { pending: Waiting, uploading: Uploading, paused: Paused, error: Upload failed, success: Uploaded } — pass only the keys you override.

events.name: update:modelValue
events.description: Emitted with the next file list when files are added, removed, or cleared.

events.name: files-accepted
events.description: Emitted with only the newly accepted files, after accept/maxFiles/maxFileSize validation.

events.name: files-rejected
events.description: Pre-acceptance validation failures. Unrelated to transport errors, which you report through `uploadState`.

events.name: retry
events.description: Emitted when a row's retry affordance is activated. Pure intent — the component performs no upload. Use `payload.mode` ('resume' | 'restart'); it reports the affordance the component ACTUALLY DREW, so never re-derive it from your own state.

events.name: cancel
events.description: Emitted ONLY for rows whose status is 'uploading' or 'paused', immediately BEFORE the `update:modelValue` that drops them — so the file is still in the list when your handler runs and you can abort the request. `reason` is 'remove' for one row or 'clear' (one event per in-flight file) for Clear all.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Replaces the dropzone icon.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Replaces the dropzone body. Receives `{ files, isDragActive, isDragReject, openFileDialog, clearFiles }`.

slots.name: file
slots.description: Full row override. Receives all of TFileUploadFileSlotProps, including every derived label, so a custom row re-derives nothing.

slots.name: file-status
slots.description: Replaces ONLY the progress bar + status line + error block inside the default row. Receives TFileUploadFileSlotProps. Name, meta, thumbnail, Retry, and Remove keep rendering. Ignored when the `file` slot is provided.

slots.name: actions
slots.description: Footer actions. Receives `{ files, openFileDialog, clearFiles }` — no upload state.

a11y: per_row_progressbar_is_labelled_with_status_and_filename: e.g. 'Uploading hero.png' — every row's bar has a distinct accessible name.
terminal_transitions_announced_with_filenames: One polite root live region (`role=log`) announces only success/error, e.g. 'alpha.txt uploaded.' / 'alpha.txt failed. Connection lost.'

repeated_identical_messages_are_still_announced: The log appends each message as a new node rather than overwriting one string, so a retry that fails with the byte-identical error still mutates the region and is announced. It is not aria-atomic: only the appended message is read, and the trimmed history (5 most recent) is not.
percentages_are_never_announced: Percent and ETA live in non-live text; the value reaches assistive tech through the progressbar's aria-valuenow.
focus_is_rescued_when_retry_unmounts: Flipping error -> uploading removes the focused Retry; focus moves to that row's Remove (or the dropzone), and only when it was genuinely lost.
behavior: upload_state_is_keyed_by_file_identity: Both source shapes key on the File object, never on index or name. Key your own store with the File objects you receive from `files-accepted`.

a_lookup_miss_renders_nothing: An unknown File yields `undefined` and the row falls back to name + meta only — no bar, no status, no retry. This fail-blank is deliberate: it is the back-compat path and it fails loudly instead of showing another file's percentage.

loading_is_presentational: BREAKING since 0.14.0 — `loading` no longer disables anything. It was freezing the dropzone, Clear all, and every Remove, which blocked removing file 1 while file 4 uploaded. Use `:disabled` to reproduce the old behavior; prefer `uploadState` and freeze nothing.
each_file_has_an_independent_track: One row's error, progress, or retry never gates another's. Remove stays enabled for in-flight rows and emits `cancel` so you can abort.
errors_are_per_row: Transport errors go in `state.error` and render under their own row. They never enter the dropzone feedback list, which Remove and Clear all wipe.
drag_feedback_has_three_signals: Border, glow, and a text swap to `dragLabel` — all three fire together, including when files are already listed.

drag_reject_detection_is_mime_only: `is-drag-reject` + `dragRejectLabel` require every `accept` token to be a MIME token; with any `.ext` token the dropzone stays neutral, because a drag exposes only `.type`. A mixed drag (one good file, one bad) is NOT rejected, and the drop is never blocked — rejected files always get the normal `files-rejected` explanation.

progress_bar_visibility: Shown on uploading/paused/error, and on success only with numeric progress. 'uploading' without progress is indeterminate; paused/error without progress is a determinate 0 — an errored bar must never animate.
the_bar_is_never_reset_on_error: It holds `state.progress` in the error tone. That retained 90% is what makes the resume promise legible.
remaining_time_strings: < 5000ms -> 'Less than 5s left'; < 60s -> 'About 12s left'; < 60min -> 'About 2 min left'; else 'About 2h left'. Negative, NaN, and Infinity render nothing.
status_line_composition: Joins the present parts with ' · ': 'Uploading · 62% · About 12s left' while uploading, 'Upload failed · 2.1 MB of 2.4 MB uploaded' on a resumable error.
thumbnails_are_client_only: Object URLs are minted after mount and revoked on removal and unmount. SSR and the hydrating render both show the extension chip, so there is no mismatch.

notes: Bind `modelValue` to `File[]`.
Use `accept`, `multiple`, and size limits in the app layer to constrain uploads.

components.name: TFormField
components.description: Label + input wrapper with error and hint slots. Provides consistent layout, labelling, and validation messaging for any form control.

props.name: label
props.description: Text content for the field label.

props.name: htmlFor
props.description: The id of the associated form control, used on the <label> for attribute.

props.name: error
props.description: Error message to display. When present, the hint is hidden and the field gets is-invalid state.

props.name: hint
props.description: Hint or help text displayed below the control. Hidden when error is present.

props.name: required
props.description: Adds a visual required indicator (*) next to the label.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Applies disabled styling (reduced opacity) to the entire field.

props.name: size
props.description: Controls the label font size.

slots.name: default
slots.description: The form control (e.g. TInput, TCheckbox, TSelect).

slots.name: label
slots.description: Custom label content. Overrides the label prop text.

slots.name: error
slots.description: Custom error content. Overrides the error prop text.

slots.name: hint
slots.description: Custom hint content. Overrides the hint prop text.

a11y: Uses native <label> element with for attribute for input association.
Error messages are wrapped in role='alert' for screen reader announcements.
Required indicator (*) is aria-hidden to avoid redundant announcements; use aria-required on the input itself.
Error takes precedence over hint to avoid conflicting messages.

useWhen: You need consistent label, hint, and error layout around a control.
avoidWhen: You need the actual input control.

alternatives.when: You need a text entry control.
You need a dropdown control.

required: Wrap a control such as `TInput`, `TSelect`, `TCombobox`, `TCheckbox`, or `TDatePicker`.
Pass `htmlFor` matching the child control `id` when the child exposes one.
notes: Set the child control invalid state separately when `error` is present.

components.name: TGrid
components.purpose: Responsive grid wrapper for cards, forms, stats, and other multi-column layouts.
a11y: Acts as a layout wrapper only; semantics come from child content and optional `as` prop.

useWhen: Children should flow into responsive columns.
You need stat grids, card grids, or form rows.
avoidWhen: You only need one-dimensional spacing.

alternatives.when: A single row or column is enough.

components.name: TIcon
components.purpose: Render a registered TreeUI icon by name with consistent sizing and accessibility defaults.

props.name: name
props.description: Icon identifier from the @treeui/icons registry.

props.name: size
props.description: Pixel size for both width and height of the rendered svg.

props.name: absoluteStrokeWidth
props.description: When true, stroke width stays visually constant across sizes.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label. When set, the icon becomes role="img" with aria-label; otherwise it is aria-hidden.

a11y: rule: Provide `label` only when the icon conveys meaning that is not already in adjacent text.

useWhen: You need to render a registered TreeUI icon by name.
You want consistent sizing and a11y defaults without importing the icon component directly.
avoidWhen: The icon is not in the @treeui/icons registry — import or register it instead.

alternatives.when: You need tree-shaken, statically-imported icon components.

components.name: TInput
components.purpose: Text input with size, loading, invalid, and prefix or suffix slots.

props.name: width
props.note: Inline-size cap (max-inline-size). Fluid by default; use instead of an inline width style.

useWhen: The value is short, plain, and single-line.
avoidWhen: The content is multiline, numeric, or markdown.

alternatives.when: The content is multiline.
The value is numeric.
Rich markdown authoring is needed.

components.name: TKbd
components.purpose: Keyboard-key hint (e.g. ⌘K, Esc, Ctrl) rendered as a native <kbd> with the library's chip styling.
a11y: Uses the native <kbd> element so assistive tech announces it as keyboard input.

components.name: TLink
components.purpose: Inline link with consistent styling, optional RouterLink rendering, and external link support.

props.name: to
props.description: Route target. When provided and vue-router is available, renders RouterLink.

props.name: href
props.description: Native href for anchor elements. Ignored when `to` is provided with vue-router.

props.name: external
props.description: Opens link in new tab with rel='noopener noreferrer'.

a11y: semantic_element: a (or RouterLink, or span when disabled)

components.name: TMarkdownEditor
components.description: A rich Markdown editor with live preview, toolbar formatting, keyboard shortcuts, and image paste/drop upload support.

props.name: modelValue
props.description: Markdown content (v-model)

props.name: uploadImage
props.description: Async function that receives a File and returns the uploaded image URL. When not provided, pasting an image inserts an empty markdown image tag.

events.name: update:modelValue
events.description: Emitted on every content change for v-model binding.

events.name: image-upload
events.description: Emitted after a successful image upload with the file and resulting URL.

slots.name: toolbar
slots.description: Extra content appended to the toolbar.

a11y: Tab buttons use role="tab" and aria-selected.
Upload status bar uses role="status" and aria-live="polite".
All toolbar buttons have title attributes describing the action.
Textarea has aria-label for screen readers.

useWhen: The user needs markdown formatting, toolbar actions, or preview.
avoidWhen: A plain multiline text area is enough.

alternatives.when: You only need multiline plain text.

components.name: TModal
components.purpose: Accessible modal dialog for focused tasks and confirmations.

useWhen: The user must complete or review something in a blocking dialog.
avoidWhen: Only a small hint or contextual panel is needed.

alternatives.when: The dialog is specifically about confirm/cancel.
A side panel fits better.
An anchored non-blocking panel is enough.

notes: Use `v-model:open` for the idiomatic Vue controlled pattern.

components.name: TMultiSelect
components.purpose: Searchable multi-select control that renders selected values as tags.

props.name: options
props.note: Array of `{ label, value, description?, keywords?, disabled? }`.

useWhen: The user needs to select multiple values from a larger list.
Filtering and tag display improve the workflow.
avoidWhen: The option set is tiny and should always stay visible.

alternatives.when: The multiple-choice set is very small and visible.
The choices can stay visible as a checklist.
The choices are hierarchical.

notes: Pass `modelValue` as `string[]`.
Option search checks `label`, `description`, and `keywords`.
Use `maxVisibleTags` to control trigger density in compact layouts.

components.name: TNavbar
components.purpose: Top application bar with start, center, and end content areas.
a11y: Use `as="nav"` or nested navigation landmarks when the app bar itself is navigation.
Acts as a structural wrapper; semantics come from `as` and child content.

useWhen: You need a top application bar with start, center, and end areas.
avoidWhen: You only need a content container.

alternatives.when: You only need width constraint.

components.name: TNavMenu
components.purpose: Vertical navigation menu with selection state, keyboard navigation, optional RouterLink rendering, and sidebar integration.

props.name: items
props.note: Array of `{ label, value, shortLabel?, description?, icon?, to?, badge?, disabled?, exact? }`.

props.name: exact
props.note: Default active-match strategy for router-link items. `true` keeps only the exactly-active route highlighted (prevents prefix matches from lighting up several items at once). Override per item via `TNavMenuItem.exact`.

a11y: aria_disabled: on link items when disabled

useWhen: You need app-section navigation inside a shell or sidebar.
avoidWhen: You need tabs for small panel switching.

alternatives.when: The navigation swaps local panels instead of app sections.
You are showing hierarchy, not selectable nav.

notes: When nested inside `TSidebar` or `TAppShell`, `collapsed` can be inherited from the sidebar provider.

components.name: TNumberInput
components.purpose: Numeric input with optional increment and decrement controls.

useWhen: The value is numeric and increment/decrement controls help.
avoidWhen: The value is free-form text or non-numeric.

alternatives.when: The value is not numeric.

components.name: TPage
components.purpose: Semantic page region under the app shell: block padding, a centered max-width content column, and standardized vertical rhythm between sections.

props.name: width
props.note: Max-width of the centered content column. Mirrors TContainer sizes.

props.name: padded
props.note: Adds vertical (block) padding on the region and horizontal padding on the inner column.

props.name: gap
props.note: Vertical spacing between direct children (e.g. a TPageHeader and content sections).

a11y: Structural wrapper only; landmark semantics come from `as` and the surrounding app shell (avoid nesting a second `main`).

useWhen: You want a standard screen region under the app shell with consistent max width, page padding, and vertical rhythm between sections.
avoidWhen: You only need a bare width constraint with no page padding or child spacing.

alternatives.when: You just need a centered max-width wrapper without page-level block padding or child spacing.
You only need to space a group of children, not lay out a whole page.

components.name: TPageHeader
components.purpose: Canonical page title block: heading, optional subtitle, and a right-aligned actions area, so title hierarchy and alignment stay consistent across screens.

props.name: title
props.note: Rendered inside the heading element. Overridden by the `title` slot.

props.name: level
props.note: Heading level for the title element. Default `1` treats it as the page title; lower it when the page already has an h1.

a11y: Title renders as a real heading element (`h1`–`h6`) chosen by `level`; keep one h1 per page.
Actions area holds standard focusable controls (e.g. TButton) — no custom roles added.

useWhen: A screen needs a title (and optional subtitle) with a right-aligned actions area in a consistent hierarchy.
avoidWhen: You need cross-page hierarchy or navigation trails rather than a single page title.

alternatives.when: You are showing where the page sits in a hierarchy, not its title.
You need a top application bar, not a per-page title block.

components.name: TPagination
components.purpose: Page controls with previous/next navigation, numbered page buttons, and ellipsis for large page ranges. Supports v-model for the active page.

props.name: modelValue
props.description: Current active page (1-based). Use with v-model.

props.name: totalPages
props.description: Total number of pages.

props.name: siblings
props.description: Number of sibling page buttons shown around the current page.

props.name: size
props.description: Size variant for the pagination controls.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables all pagination controls.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label for the nav element.

events.name: update:modelValue
events.description: Emitted when the active page changes. Enables v-model binding.

slots.name: previous
slots.description: Custom content for the previous button.

slots.name: next
slots.description: Custom content for the next button.

a11y: aria_label: Configurable via label prop, defaults to 'Pagination'
aria_current_page: Applied to the active page button
disabled_buttons: Previous disabled on first page, next disabled on last page
keyboard_support: ["Tab/Shift+Tab to navigate between buttons","Enter/Space to activate a page button"]
focus_visible: box-shadow focus ring on all interactive buttons
behavior: ellipsis: Shown when page range exceeds visible siblings around the current page
clamping: Page value is clamped between 1 and totalPages
no_emit_on_same_page: Does not emit when clicking the already-active page

useWhen: The app handles paging outside of `TTable`.
avoidWhen: The list is infinite-scroll or not paged.

alternatives.when: The interaction is switching panels, not pages.

components.name: TPopover
components.description: Rich floating content panel anchored to a trigger element. Opened by click, dismissed by outside click or Escape. Supports four placement sides with alignment options.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled open state (v-model:open)

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Initial open state for uncontrolled usage

props.name: disabled
props.description: Prevents opening

props.name: side
props.description: Placement side relative to trigger

props.name: align
props.description: Alignment along the placement axis

props.name: id
props.description: Custom id for the content panel

events.name: update:open
events.description: Emitted when open state changes (enables v-model:open)

events.name: open-change
events.description: Emitted when open state changes (non-v-model usage)

slots.name: trigger
slots.description: Custom trigger element. Receives current open state.

slots.name: default
slots.description: Popover content displayed inside the floating panel.

a11y: attrs: ["aria-haspopup=\"dialog\" on trigger","aria-expanded on trigger","aria-controls linking trigger to content","tabindex=\"-1\" on content for focus management"]
keyboard: ["Click or Enter/Space on trigger toggles popover","Escape closes popover and restores focus to trigger","Tab navigates within popover content"]
focus: ["On open, focuses first focusable element inside content (or content itself)","On Escape close, restores focus to trigger"]
dismiss: ["Outside click closes popover","Escape key closes popover"]

useWhen: You need custom anchored content that can contain controls.
avoidWhen: The content is just a short hint.

alternatives.when: Only a short hint or label is needed.
The content is primarily a menu of actions.

components.name: TPricing
components.description: Grid wrapper that renders multiple PricingCard components from an array of plan objects.

props.name: plans
props.description: Array of plan objects. Each plan supports the same props as PricingCard (title, description, price, currency, period, features, highlighted, badge, buttonText, disabled).

props.name: size
props.description: Size applied to all child pricing cards.

props.name: columns
props.description: Number of grid columns. Defaults to the number of plans.

events.name: select
events.description: Emitted when any plan's CTA is clicked. Provides the plan data and index.

slots.name: plan
slots.description: Custom rendering for each plan card.

a11y: Each plan is wrapped in role="listitem".

notes: Pass `plans` matching the same core fields used by `TPricingCard`.

components.name: TPricingCard
components.description: A card component for displaying pricing plans with title, price, features list, and call-to-action button. Supports highlighted state and badges.

props.name: title
props.description: Plan name displayed in the card header.

props.name: description
props.description: Short plan description below the title.

props.name: price
props.description: The price value to display.

props.name: currency
props.description: Currency symbol displayed before the price.

props.name: period
props.description: Billing period text (e.g., "/month", "/year").

props.name: features
props.description: Array of features to display. Each feature has `text: string` and optional `included: boolean` (defaults to true). Excluded features are shown with strikethrough.

props.name: highlighted
props.description: When true, the card gets a prominent border and shadow to indicate the recommended plan.

props.name: badge
props.description: Badge text displayed above the card (e.g., "Most popular").

props.name: buttonText
props.description: CTA button label. If omitted and no action slot, footer is hidden.

props.name: size
props.description: Card size — affects padding, font sizes.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the CTA button.

events.name: select
events.description: Emitted when the CTA button is clicked.

slots.name: header
slots.description: Custom header content replacing title and description.

slots.name: price
slots.description: Custom price display replacing currency, amount, and period.

slots.name: features
slots.description: Custom features list replacing the built-in feature list.

slots.name: action
slots.description: Custom CTA area replacing the built-in button.

slots.name: badge
slots.description: Custom badge content replacing the built-in badge.

a11y: CTA button inherits TButton a11y (disabled, aria-busy).

components.name: TProgress
components.description: Determinate and indeterminate progress indicator. Supports linear bar and circular ring variants.

props.name: value
props.description: Current progress value (0–max). Omit for indeterminate.

props.name: max
props.description: Maximum value.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label read by screen readers.

a11y: notes: Indeterminate state omits aria-valuenow so assistive tech announces the bar as indeterminate.

components.name: TRadio
components.purpose: Single radio option within a TRadioGroup; renders a native radio input.

components.name: TRadioGroup
components.purpose: Container for TRadio options; manages v-model for single-select from a group.
a11y: keyboard: Arrow keys move between radios within the group

components.name: TSelect
components.purpose: Custom styled dropdown select with listbox; controlled and uncontrolled via v-model and open/defaultOpen.

props.name: options
props.note: Array of { label: string; value: string | number; disabled?: boolean }

props.name: width
props.note: Inline-size cap (max-inline-size). Fluid by default; use instead of an inline width style.

useWhen: The user needs a compact single-choice dropdown.
Search is not required.
avoidWhen: Filtering or multi-selection is required.

alternatives.when: Search or filtering is required.
Multiple values are required.

notes: Pass `options` as `{ label, value, disabled? }[]` where `value` can be `string` or `number`.
Use `placeholder` when no selection has been made.

components.name: TSelectableList
components.purpose: Rich single-selection list for options with description and metadata.

props.name: items
props.note: Array of `{ label, value, description?, meta?, disabled? }`.

useWhen: Each option needs description or metadata.
Selection should feel like choosing a row rather than opening a dropdown.
avoidWhen: The options should live in a compact form control.

alternatives.when: A compact dropdown is better.
The options are hierarchical.

components.name: TSidebar
components.purpose: Persistent or collapsible side shell for app navigation and secondary workspace controls.

useWhen: You need a persistent side shell, often with navigation.
avoidWhen: The panel should slide over temporary content.

alternatives.when: The panel is temporary and overlays content.

notes: Pair with `TNavMenu` for app-section navigation.
Use controlled `collapsed` state when the app shell persists layout preferences.
Prefer `TAppShell` when you also want full-viewport sizing, a top header, and a responsive mobile drawer.

components.name: TSkeleton
components.purpose: Placeholder shimmer for loading states. Renders an animated block that indicates content is being loaded.

props.name: width
props.description: CSS width of the skeleton element.

props.name: height
props.description: CSS height of the skeleton element.

props.name: animation
props.description: Animation style for the shimmer effect.

slots.name: default
slots.description: When provided, skeleton wraps children and hides them while loading prop is true.

a11y: notes: Skeleton is purely decorative. Use aria-busy on the parent container.

components.name: TSpacer
components.purpose: Flexible spacer that absorbs free space between siblings in a flex layout (e.g. TStack), replacing hand-written margin:auto / flex:1.
a11y: Rendered with aria-hidden='true'; it is a layout-only element with no content.

components.name: TSparkline
components.purpose: Tiny, axis-less inline trend chart (line, area, or bar) sized for stat tiles, table cells, and running text.

props.name: color
props.note: Any CSS color.

props.name: smooth
props.note: Curved line/area.

props.name: strokeWidth
props.note: Line/area only.

props.name: showLastPoint
props.note: Marker on the final point (line/area).

a11y: Renders role=img with an aria-label; pass a specific one for meaningful trends.
Pair with a visible number — a sparkline supplements a value, it does not replace it.

useWhen: You want a compact trend beside a value, in a table cell, or inline in text.
No axes, legend, or tooltip are required.
avoidWhen: You need axes, multiple labeled series, or a tooltip — use TChart.

alternatives.when: The chart needs axes, a legend, or a tooltip.
A single value with a trend badge is enough.

notes: Pass `data` as `number[]`; there are no axes, legend, or tooltip by design.
Size it with `width`/`height`; use it inside `TStat`, table cells, or text.
Always keep a visible number nearby — a sparkline supplements a value, it does not replace it.

components.name: TSpinner
components.purpose: Accessible loading indicator.

components.name: TSplit
components.purpose: Responsive two-pane layout (a wider main pane + a narrower aside) that sits side by side and stacks to one column when narrow — no media query.

props.name: ratio
props.note: How many times faster the main pane grows than the aside pane.

props.name: minWidth
props.note: Min width each pane keeps before the layout wraps to a single column (flex-wrap, no @media).

props.name: side
props.note: Which side the aside pane sits on when side by side.

props.name: align
props.note: Cross-axis alignment (align-items).

a11y: Layout-only wrapper; semantics come from the slotted content.

components.name: TStack
components.purpose: One-dimensional spacing and alignment wrapper for vertical or horizontal compositions.

props.name: grow
props.note: Expands the stack to fill available space along its parent's main axis (flex: 1 with min-size 0). Use it to make a sidebar column fill its container height.

a11y: Acts as a layout wrapper only; semantics come from `as` and children.

useWhen: You need one-dimensional spacing and alignment.
avoidWhen: You need responsive columns.

alternatives.when: You need multi-column layout.

components.name: TStackItem
components.purpose: Per-child flex control inside a TStack — grow, shrink, basis, min width and align-self — so one item can fill the row without hand-written CSS.

props.name: grow
props.note: true = flex-grow 1; a number sets the grow factor.

props.name: shrink
props.note: false prevents shrinking; a number sets the shrink factor.

props.name: basis
props.note: flex-basis (initial main-axis size).

props.name: minWidth
props.note: Minimum main-axis size before it wraps/shrinks.

props.name: align
props.note: Per-item cross-axis alignment (align-self).

a11y: Layout-only wrapper; semantics come from the slotted content.

components.name: TStat
components.purpose: Compact KPI display with label, value, trend, metadata, and optional icon.
a11y: Use clear label text so the metric remains understandable to assistive technologies.
Trend indicator symbols are decorative when slot content already explains the change.

useWhen: A compact metric card with value, trend, and optional icon is needed.
avoidWhen: You need arbitrary content in a generic surface.

alternatives.when: The content is not a metric.
You want to show the metric's trend as an inline mini-chart.

components.name: TSteps
components.purpose: Stepper component for visualizing workflow progress, with optional interactive selection.

props.name: items
props.note: Array of `{ label, value, description?, meta?, status?, disabled? }`.

useWhen: A workflow or staged process should be visible.
avoidWhen: You need generic tab panels or route navigation.

alternatives.when: The interaction is panel switching rather than process progression.

notes: Pass explicit `status` per item only when the app should override the computed complete/current/upcoming logic.

components.name: TSwitch
components.purpose: On/off toggle; semantic alternative to checkbox for settings. Supports v-model via modelValue.

useWhen: The state is a single immediate boolean toggle.
avoidWhen: The choice is one of multiple discrete options.

alternatives.when: The control participates in a form checklist or consent flow.
The user is choosing from multiple options.

components.name: TTable
components.description: Sortable, accessible data table with column definitions and row data.

props.name: columns
props.description: Array of column definitions with key, label, sortable, align, and width.

props.name: rows
props.description: Array of row data objects keyed by column keys.

props.name: sortBy
props.description: Controlled sort state. When provided, overrides internal sort tracking.

events.name: sort
events.description: Emitted when a sortable column header is clicked.

slots.name: cell-{key}
slots.description: Custom cell renderer for a specific column. Receives `{ row, value }`.

slots.name: header-{key}
slots.description: Custom header renderer for a specific column. Receives `{ column }`.

slots.name: empty
slots.description: Content shown when rows array is empty.

a11y: aria-sort: Set on sortable column headers (ascending, descending, none).
keyboard: Sortable headers are focusable and activated with Enter or Space.
scope: col on all th elements.

useWhen: The content is tabular and benefits from rows and columns.
avoidWhen: The UI is hierarchical or card-based.

alternatives.when: The list is rich but not tabular.
The data is hierarchical.

components.name: TTabs
components.purpose: Tabbed panel navigation with full keyboard support and automatic/manual activation modes.

props.name: modelValue
props.note: The value of the currently active tab. Use with v-model.

props.name: defaultValue
props.note: Initial active tab when uncontrolled.

props.name: activationMode
props.note: automatic: tab activates on focus. manual: tab activates on Enter/Space.

props.name: disabled
props.note: Disables every tab in the list.

useWhen: The user switches between a small set of panels in-place.
avoidWhen: The panels represent steps in a workflow or app navigation.

alternatives.when: The panels represent a process.
The selection is app-section navigation.

components.name: TTag
components.description: Removable label/chip for filters and selections.

props.name: removable
props.description: Shows a remove/close button.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and dims the tag.

events.name: remove
events.description: Emitted when the remove button is clicked (not emitted when disabled).

slots.name: default
slots.description: Tag label content.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Optional leading icon.

a11y: remove_button: Has aria-label="Remove" and is keyboard-focusable.
disabled_state: Applied via is-disabled class and native disabled on remove button.

components.name: TText
components.purpose: Typography primitive for body text, labels and headings — size, tone, weight and truncation from tokens, with no hand-written CSS.

props.name: as
props.note: Render element. Use `p`, `h1`–`h6`, `label`, `small`, `strong`, … for correct semantics.

props.name: size
props.note: Maps to --tree-font-size-*. Omitted = inherit.

props.name: tone
props.note: `default` inherits the current colour; others map to --tree-color-text-* / brand.

props.name: truncate
props.note: Clip to a single line with an ellipsis.

a11y: Choose `as` to convey the correct semantics (e.g. headings via h1–h6); TText adds only visual styling.

components.name: TTextarea
components.purpose: Multi-line text field with size, loading, invalid states, and auto-grow option.

props.name: width
props.note: Inline-size cap (max-inline-size). Fluid by default; use instead of an inline width style.

useWhen: The value is multiline plain text.
avoidWhen: The authoring experience requires markdown formatting.

alternatives.when: The value is short and single-line.
Markdown authoring is needed.

components.name: TTimeline
components.purpose: Vertical timeline for activity history, audits, release events, and chronological product updates.

props.name: items
props.note: Array of { id?: string | number; title: string; description?: string; meta?: string; timestamp?: string; datetime?: string; tone?: 'neutral' | 'brand' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' }

a11y: root_semantics: Renders as an ordered list (`ol`) by default.

components.name: TToast
components.purpose: Ephemeral notification stack driven by a composable. Renders toast items inside a positioned container with auto-dismiss and manual close.

useWhen: Feedback should appear temporarily without shifting layout.
avoidWhen: The message must stay visible in-flow.

alternatives.when: The message should stay visible in the page.

required: Mount one `TToastProvider` near the application root before calling `useToast()` from feature components.
notes: Use `useToast()` for programmatic notifications.
Choose provider `position` once per app shell unless a feature has a strong reason to differ.

components.name: TToggleGroup
components.purpose: Segmented button group for single or multiple visible choices.

props.name: options
props.note: Array of `{ label, value, disabled? }`.

useWhen: The option set is small and should stay visible.
A segmented-button presentation is appropriate.
avoidWhen: The option set is large or searchable.

alternatives.when: A compact dropdown fits better.
Filtering is required.
Many multiple-choice options are required.

notes: Use `selectionMode="single"` for segmented single choice.
Use `selectionMode="multiple"` for small visible multi-choice sets.

components.name: TTooltip
components.purpose: Small contextual overlay shown on hover or focus.

useWhen: You need a short, non-interactive hint anchored to another element.
avoidWhen: The overlay needs interactive content.

alternatives.when: The overlay needs interactive content.

components.name: TTreeView
components.purpose: Hierarchical tree view with expansion, keyboard navigation, and single or multiple selection.

props.name: nodes
props.note: Array of `{ id, label, description?, meta?, disabled?, children? }`.

useWhen: The data is hierarchical.
Selection or expansion must work across nested nodes.
avoidWhen: The data is flat.

alternatives.when: The options are flat rich rows.
The hierarchy is actually app navigation.

notes: Use `nodes` shaped as `{ id, label, description?, meta?, disabled?, children? }[]`.
Control `expanded` separately when the parent app needs to persist open branches.
